item.title: [NBJ] I Looked At Amazon After They Fired 16,000 Engineers. Their AI Broke Everything.
item.itunes:title: I Looked At Amazon After They Fired 16,000 Engineers. Their AI Broke Everything.
item.description:
What's really happening inside your codebase when AI writes code nobody fully understands?


The common story is that dark code is a security or engineering quality problem. But the reality is more complicated: it's an organizational capability crisis that is only going to get worse.


In this video, I share the inside scoop on dark code and what actually fixes it:


вЂў Why observability and agent pipelines don't solve the core problem

вЂў How spec-driven development forces comprehension before code exists

вЂў What self-describing systems look like and why they matter at AI speed

вЂў Where a comprehension gate catches what the first two layers miss


Every builder, founder, and engineering leader shipping AI-generated code right now faces a choice: treat dark code as an organizational discipline problem, or keep driving with the headlights off.


Subscribe for daily AI strategy and news. For playbooks and analysis: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/your-codebase-is-full-of-code-nobody?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: [MoPod] DMF MXL вЂ” Legacy, Software Production, and the Dynamic Media Facility
item.description: Three answers on why legacy infrastructure cannot flex, what software-defined production has already achieved, and why DMF with MXL shared memory is the next step.

item.title: [AIDB] Harness Engineering 101
item.description:
We went from prompt engineering to context engineering, and now the discipline everyone in AI is talking about is harness engineering вЂ” designing the systems, tools, and context you put around a model so it can actually do real work. Today's episode is a primer on what harness engineering is, why it explains the strange convergence of every AI product into the same shape, and what Anthropic's new managed agents tell us about where it's all heading.

item.title: [MoPod] DMF & MXL v3 вЂ” Legacy Strain, Software Production & The Fabric
item.description: Updated deep dive: why legacy infrastructure is holding us back, the need for change, what software production is and how we are already using it, then into DMF/MXL architecture, workflows, ecosystem, and honest gaps.

item.description: SOM Open Specification v0.2 walked through in sixteen minutes. Architecture, six properties, Skills specification, federated identity, vendor tiers, TAMS junction, and three blind spots. Includes diagram callouts.

item.title: [NBJ] I Watched 3 Companies Lay Off Their Managers. All 3 Hit the Same Wall.
item.itunes:title: I Watched 3 Companies Lay Off Their Managers. All 3 Hit the Same Wall.
item.description:
What's really happening inside the management layer your company just removed вЂ” and why it matters more than anyone is admitting?


The common story is that flatter is faster вЂ” but the reality is more complicated: companies are cutting load-bearing structure without understanding what they're actually removing.


In this video, I share the inside scoop on how to unbundle management in the age of AI:


вЂў Why management breaks into three jobs AI handles very differently

вЂў How Kimi, Block, and Meta are running three distinct real-world experiments

вЂў What gets lost when you compress instead of decompose the management role

вЂў Where human judgment stays irreplaceable even as LLMs scale


Operators and leaders who take the time to decompose what managers actually do вЂ” before automating or eliminating вЂ” will build more durable, higher-performing teams than those who simply cut and compress.


Chapters

00:00 Introduction: The Management Removal Wave

01:30 What Do Managers Actually Do?

03:00 Bundle One: Information Routing

05:00 The Roman Legions to Railroads Through-Line

06:30 Where AI Takes Over Routing

08:00 Bundle Two: Sense Making

10:30 Why Sense Making Resists Automation

12:30 Bundle Three: Accountability and Feedback

14:30 What Happens If AI Gets 10x Better?

16:00 Case Study One: Kimi's Radical Flat Structure

19:00 Case Study Two: Jack Dorsey and Block's DRI Model

22:00 Case Study Three: Meta's Compression Play

25:30 What This Means for Managers and ICs

27:00 The Decomposition Playbook


Subscribe for daily AI strategy and news.


For deeper playbooks and analysis: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/


Full Story w/ Prompts: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/executive-briefing-44-of-companies

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: [AIDB] The New AI Org Chart
item.description:
Jack Dorsey and Sequoia's Roelof Botha published a sweeping essay arguing that AI can replace the information-routing function of hierarchy itself вЂ” and Block is betting the company on it. Today we read through their vision for the "company as intelligence," then compare it to the messy, bottom-up reality emerging at Every, where agents are already forming a shadow org chart.

item.itunes:summary:
Jack Dorsey and Sequoia's Roelof Botha published a sweeping essay arguing that AI can replace the information-routing function of hierarchy itself вЂ” and Block is betting the company on it. Today we read through their vision for the "company as intelligence," then compare it to the messy, bottom-up reality emerging at Every, where agents are already forming a shadow org chart.

item.title: [NBJ] Google's New Quantization is a Game Changer
item.itunes:title: Google's New Quantization is a Game Changer
item.description:
What's really happening inside AI memory, and why it's the bottleneck threatening every LLM deployment at scale?


The common story is that we just need more chips, but the reality is more interesting: a new Google paper may have just changed the math without touching the hardware.


In this video, I share the inside scoop on TurboQuant, Google's lossless KV cache compression breakthrough:


вЂў Why the AI memory crisis is structural, not temporary

вЂў How TurboQuant achieves 6x compression with zero data loss

вЂў What lossless KV cache optimization means for LLM architecture

вЂў Where Google, NVIDIA, and enterprises each stand to win or lose


The operators and builders who start treating memory as a years-long constraint, and take control of their own context layers now, will hold a real structural advantage as this rolls toward production.


Subscribe for daily AI strategy and news. For playbooks and analysis:https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/your-gpus-just-got-6x-more-valuable?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
